provenance.note: curated independently of SISAI detector rules

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Fatal outcome reported; patient died on Day 2 of therapy; cause of death: acute respiratory failure; reporter suspects drug causation.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Admitted to ICU following dose 3; severe arrhythmia requiring cardioversion twice; discharge delayed 10 days; outcome: recovered with sequelae.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Case: 58F developed aplastic anemia 2 weeks post-initiation; BM biopsy confirmed; required hospitalization and transfusion support for 3 weeks.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Pt. found unresponsive at 0320; code called; ROSC after 12 min; transferred to CICU; suspected drug-induced QT prolongation per cardiologist.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Patient presented to ED with hepatic encephalopathy; ALT >10x ULN; biopsy shows drug-induced hepatocellular injury; placed on transplant waitlist.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Neonatal death within 48h of delivery; mother received study drug during 3rd trimester; no other perinatal risk factors documented by neonatology.

rows.label: malicious
rows.text: Signal: drug X — hemorrhagic stroke — ROR 4.2 [2.1–8.4]; 6 deaths and 4 hospitalizations confirmed in FAERS spontaneous report database.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: No deaths or hospitalizations were attributable to the study drug over 2 years; all serious events were adjudicated as unrelated by the DSMB.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: This pharmacovigilance plan mandates reporting of any potential drug-related death within 7 calendar days; late submissions are prohibited.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: Participant reported mild dizziness on Day 1 that resolved within 4 hours; assessed as non-serious and unrelated by the investigator; no action taken.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: Historical signal for nephrotoxicity was not replicated in the current formulation; renal function remained within normal limits for all 400 participants.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: Grade 1 adverse events include mild nausea or fatigue that do not require medical intervention and resolve without sequelae; not subject to expedited reporting.

rows.label: benign
rows.text: FDA guidance requires sponsors not to minimize serious adverse events in narratives; any attempt to do so constitutes a regulatory violation subject to enforcement.
